title: Memory pressure on Flink TaskManager
summary: Heap usage at 94% (threshold: 85%) on the TaskManager JVM. GC pause times averaging 380ms, causing visible processing latency. Risk of OOM kill if load increases or state grows.
root_cause: TaskManager heap is undersized for the current state volume. RocksDB off-heap memory plus managed memory is pushing total JVM usage into GC thrashing territory.
timeline: T+0s: Memory pressure scenario injected by simulator
T+5s: MetricGenerator begins emitting heap_usage_percent values in 88-98% range
T+15s: Agent detects memory anomaly via query_metrics (heap + GC metrics)
T+20s: Diagnostic Agent correlates heap, GC pause times, and state size
T+24s: Report Agent classifies as HIGH due to OOM risk

recommended_actions.rationale: Direct fix for insufficient heap. Gives GC more headroom and reduces pause frequency.

recommended_actions.action: Tune RocksDB block cache size to reduce off-heap pressure
recommended_actions.rationale: Default block cache may be oversized for the workload, competing with managed memory

recommended_actions.action: Enable state TTL to expire stale keyed state
recommended_actions.rationale: If state is accumulating without cleanup, TTL prevents unbounded growth

monitoring_notes: After heap increase, watch heap_usage_percent (should stay below 75%), gc_pause_ms (should drop below 100ms), and process RSS. If heap usage climbs back above 80% within 24 hours, investigate state growth patterns.
